questions.question: Why did Rakesh’s parents send him to live with his grandfather?
questions.explanation: Rakesh stayed with his grandfather so he could attend school in Mussoorie.

questions.question: Where did Rakesh buy the cherries?
questions.explanation: Rakesh bought cherries from the Mussoorie bazaar.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh decide to plant the cherry seed?
questions.explanation: Grandfather encouraged Rakesh to plant the seed instead of keeping it.

questions.question: What was the first sign that the cherry seed had begun to grow?
questions.explanation: Rakesh noticed a small twig growing from the soil.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh put pebbles around the plant?
questions.explanation: The pebbles protected the small plant from damage.

questions.question: What damage did the goat cause to the cherry plant?
questions.explanation: The goat ate all the leaves of the young cherry tree.

questions.question: What caused further damage to the cherry tree after the goat incident?
questions.explanation: A woman cutting grass accidentally cut the tree with her scythe.

questions.question: How did the cherry tree show resilience after being damaged?
questions.explanation: New shoots and leaves appeared, showing the tree’s resilience.

questions.question: Which insect did Rakesh notice sitting on the cherry tree?
questions.explanation: A bright green praying mantis was seen on the tree.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh remove the caterpillar from the tree?
questions.explanation: The caterpillar was eating the leaves of the plant.

questions.question: Which season helped the cherry tree grow faster?
questions.explanation: The monsoon rains helped the tree grow quickly.

questions.question: What appeared on the cherry tree after several years?
questions.explanation: A pale pink blossom appeared on the tree.

questions.question: How did Rakesh feel when he saw the blossom?
questions.explanation: He was amazed and proud of the tree’s growth.

questions.question: Which birds came to eat the cherries later?
questions.explanation: Bulbuls and scarlet minivets came to eat the cherries.

questions.question: Why was the cherry tree special to Rakesh?

options.B: He planted and cared for it himself

questions.explanation: Rakesh had planted the seed and watched it grow.

questions.question: What does the cherry tree mainly symbolize in the story?
questions.explanation: The tree symbolizes patience, growth, and nurturing nature.

questions.question: What kind of relationship did Rakesh share with his grandfather?
questions.explanation: Grandfather guided and supported Rakesh.

questions.question: What natural sounds were heard at night in the forest?
questions.explanation: The forest was filled with the sounds of insects.

questions.question: What did Rakesh learn from planting the cherry tree?

options.B: Patience and care are needed for growth

questions.explanation: The story shows that patience and care help things grow.

questions.question: What feeling did Rakesh experience after seeing the tree grow?
questions.explanation: He felt a sense of wonder at creating life from a seed.

questions.question: Who wrote the story about the cherry tree?
questions.explanation: The story was written by Ruskin Bond.

questions.question: Where did Rakesh plant the seed?
questions.explanation: The seed was planted in the garden near his grandfather’s house.

questions.question: What does the story mainly highlight?
questions.explanation: The story emphasizes nurturing nature and patience.

questions.question: What helped the cherry tree survive after damage?
questions.explanation: The tree survived because of its natural resilience and Rakesh’s care.

questions.question: What is the main idea of the story?
questions.explanation: The story focuses on nurturing a seed into a tree with patience and care.
